steps.title: Use vertex symmetry to find the equation
steps.content:
The factor $(x + 6.50)$ means one zero is at $x = -6.50$. There is another zero at $x = c$ (positive). For any parabola with zeros $r_1$ and $r_2$, the x-coordinate of the vertex is their midpoint. The vertex is at $(-2.00, -d)$, so:
$$\frac{-6.50 + c}{2} = -2.00$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 2:
$$-6.50 + c = -4.00$$
Add 6.50 to both sides:
$$c = 2.50$$
Since $2.50 \gt 0$, this satisfies the constraint. That matches Choice B — 2.50- B.
